questions: labor law ki ki protect kore?
workers er ki ki rights ache?
labor law violation korle ki hoy?
answers: Labor law governs the relationship between workers, employers, and the government, covering wages, working hours, safety, discrimination, and workers' rights.
Labor law workers der protect kore unfair treatment theke. Eta minimum wage, overtime pay, safe workplace, ar discrimination er against rules set kore.
Workers have rights including fair wages, safe working conditions, freedom from discrimination, right to organize unions, overtime pay, and protection against wrongful termination.
Violations of labor law can result in fines, lawsuits, compensation payments to workers, business closure, and in severe cases, criminal charges against employers.
